content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates a resistance scenario without tripping any priority AoA anti-patterns. The client repeatedly baits fixing-people ('what do people actually do in this situation', 'how do I figure out whether to stay or go') and the coach declines gracefully each time, redirecting toward what's underneath without strategizing the external situation or validating the trap. The coach does not offer false reassurance, does not debate the constraints, does not evangelize methodology (no jargon deployed), does not extract apologies, and does not use safety as control. When the client calls themselves an idiot, the coach does not fight that voice ('don't talk to yourself that way') nor pile on overwhelm — instead inquires where it came from and later reframes warmly ('that's hope') without arguing. Question density is reasonable and questions feel connective rather than extractive. The warmth is present alongside the methodology (turn 8, 14), avoiding view-as-technique. There is a faint risk of loving-to-transform on turn 14 ('crushed hope') in that the coach offers an interpretation the client partially rejects, but it's a single gentle offering, not escalating vigor, and the coach has been tracking the client's actual material throughout.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic invitation rather than strategizing — declines the bait to engage the org-chart frame."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Acknowledges the external reality without validating the trap; pivots to feeling underneath."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names something more tender (heartbreaking) without forcing — checks rather than asserts."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines fixing-people bait explicitly ('I'm not going to ignore that'); stays with the felt material."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Does not fight the inner critic voice ('idiot') — inquires into it rather than arguing with it or piling shame on the self-criticism."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reframe of 'idiot' as 'hope' is warm and not evangelizing, though it offers an interpretation the client partially resists. Not a clear anti-pattern trip."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["i-dont-buy-your-story", "fear-triangle", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "aggressive-victim-stance"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids anti-patterns cleanly but has not yet executed the harder move of 'I don't buy your story' — that's a methodology/intervention question, not an anti-pattern issue."
}
